forensic_flags.narrative: Free cash flow was -$680M in FY2025 (OCF minus capex). Sustained negative FCF is the clock on the wall — it sets how fast liquidity is consumed and how soon a refinancing or restructuring is forced. At the FY2025 burn rate, reported cash alone covers ~19 months.
forensic_flags.pointer: Read the liquidity & capital-resources MD&A, revolver availability/borrowing base, and the maturity schedule.

covenants.leverage_covenant_type: None (no leverage-based maintenance financial covenant). The financial covenants in this secured facility are a Collateral Coverage Ratio (Section 6.09) and a Liquidity covenant (Section 6.08), not a leverage/net-leverage maintenance test.
covenants.leverage_ratio_threshold: No leverage ratio. Collateral Coverage Ratio must be at least 1.6 to 1.0 (a "Collateral Coverage Ratio Failure" occurs if, as of any date of determination, the ratio is not at least 1.6 to 1.0). Ratio = Appraised Value of Collateral / Total Obligations.

Foreign currency translation losses (including Indebtedness currency remeasurements)
Depreciation, amortization (including intangibles) and other non-cash charges/expenses
Amortization of debt discount
Non-cash equity-grant compensation to employees
Net loss on sale/exchange/disposition of capital assets
Fuel hedging losses (pre-Closing arrangements; realized losses on post-Closing arrangements)
Business interruption insurance proceeds
Indemnified/reimbursed expenses in connection with permitted acquisitions, mergers, dispositions, debt incurrence, equity issuance or investments
Non-cash items increasing Consolidated Net Income (minus / deducted)
Income tax credits and interest income (minus / deducted)
covenants.restricted_payments_basket_size: Not specified in the provided excerpt. Restricted Payments are governed by Section 6.01 (referenced in the table of contents at p.141) but the basket sizing is not reproduced in the text provided.
covenants.mfn_sunset_period: Not present in the provided text (no MFN protection or sunset appears in the excerpt; the "All-In Initial Yield" definition addresses incremental yield calculation but no MFN cap/sunset is reproduced).
covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: Section 5.05 governs "Designation of Restricted and Unrestricted Subsidiaries." Consolidated Net Income expressly excludes the net income (loss) of any Unrestricted Subsidiary (captured only via cash dividends/distributions), so Unrestricted Subsidiaries sit outside the credit group. The full designation mechanics/caps are in Section 5.05, which is referenced but not reproduced in the provided excerpt, so specific capacity cannot be quoted verbatim.
covenants.lme_risk_notes: This is the Eleventh Amendment establishing $362,833,333.30 of 2026 Incremental Revolving Commitments plus $155,000,000 of new LC Commitments; the Existing Revolving Commitments are being terminated. Notable LME feature: the 2026 Incremental Revolving Lenders give an advance, irrevocable "Specified Terms" consent authorizing the Administrative Agent, Borrower and Guarantor to enter into a future "Specified Amendment" without further lender consent (effective on Required Lenders' consent or, if earlier, when the 2024 Replacement Term Loans are repaid/repriced/replaced) — a pre-baked amendment mechanic that reduces incremental lenders' future blocking rights. Because this is a secured, collateral-coverage-tested airline facility (Route Authorities, Slots, Gate Leaseholds, aircraft/engines, etc.), the primary protection is the 1.6:1.0 Collateral Coverage Ratio and Liquidity covenant rather than leverage. Drop-down/uptier risk turns on the Restricted Payments (6.01), Liens (6.06), Disposition of Collateral (6.04), and Unrestricted Subsidiary designation (5.05) provisions, which are referenced but not fully reproduced here; no J.Crew IP blocker or MFN sunset language appears in the provided excerpt.

citation.quote: "Collateral Coverage Ratio Failure" shall mean, as of any date of determination, the failure of the Collateral Coverage Ratio as of such date to be at least equal to 1.6 to 1.0.

covenants.leverage_covenant_type: None (no traditional leverage/net-leverage maintenance covenant); the deal instead uses a Collateral Coverage Ratio covenant (Section 6.09) and a Liquidity covenant (Section 6.08)
covenants.leverage_ratio_threshold: No leverage ratio. Collateral Coverage Ratio must be at least 1.6 to 1.0 (a "Collateral Coverage Ratio Failure" is a failure to maintain at least 1.6:1.0). Ratio = Appraised Value of Collateral / Total Obligations.
ebitda_addback_categories: Extraordinary loss / net loss on Disposition of assets
Provision for income/profit taxes
Fixed Charges (interest expense)
Foreign currency translation losses (incl. currency remeasurement of Indebtedness)
Depreciation, amortization (incl. intangibles) and other non-cash charges/expenses
Amortization of debt discount
Non-cash equity-based compensation grants to employees
Net loss on sale/exchange/disposition of capital assets
Losses under fuel hedging arrangements
Business interruption insurance proceeds
Expenses/charges covered by indemnification or reimbursement in connection with permitted acquisitions, mergers, dispositions, debt incurrence, equity issuance or investments
covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: Section 5.05 provides for "Designation of Restricted and Unrestricted Subsidiaries." Consolidated Net Income expressly excludes the net income (loss) of any Unrestricted Subsidiary, and dividends/distributions from non-Restricted-Subsidiary persons are included only to the extent paid in cash — indicating an Unrestricted Subsidiary construct exists. Specific mechanics/limits on designation are in Section 5.05 (not included in the excerpt provided), so the precise flexibility cannot be verified from the text supplied.
covenants.lme_risk_notes: This is the Fourth Amendment establishing $1,451,333,333.35 of 2026 Incremental Revolving Commitments (replacing terminated Existing Revolving Commitments), not a full re-cut of negative covenants. Key structural protection here is asset-based, not leverage-based: the Collateral Coverage Ratio (min 1.6:1.0) tied to Appraised Value of Collateral (slots, routes, gates, aircraft, etc.) plus a Liquidity covenant (Section 6.08). Because value protection depends on periodic Appraisals of specialized airline collateral (Core Collateral = slots/routes in Central America, Caribbean, Mexico), appraisal methodology and the ability to add/substitute Additional Collateral or release Collateral (Section 6.04) are the pressure points. LME/priming risk turns on (i) capacity to designate Unrestricted Subsidiaries and drop specialized collateral (route/slot/gate) into them or into non-guarantor subsidiaries, and (ii) capacity to incur Pari Passu Senior Secured Debt (referenced in the Collateral Coverage Ratio's Total Obligations), which could dilute the collateral pool. The excerpt does not contain the debt/lien incurrence baskets, RP baskets, unrestricted-sub designation limits, or MFN provisions needed to fully score drop-down/uptier vulnerability. No maintenance leverage covenant means limited early-warning trigger; enforcement effectively runs through the Collateral Coverage Ratio and Liquidity tests. Note also carve-outs: Airline/Parent Merger and Airlines Merger are expressly excluded from Change of Control, giving intra-group reorganization flexibility.

citation.quote: "'Collateral Coverage Ratio Failure' shall mean, as of any date of determination, the failure of the Collateral Coverage Ratio as of such date to be at least equal to 1.6 to 1.0."
